text:
Abstract

Background: The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) was designed to examine the patient’s perception of the symptomatology of supraventricular tachycardia (SVT) and its influence on quality of life. Until now there has not been a validated version for Poland, which has made impossible an analysis of the quality of life in these patients in Poland.

**Title:** Polish version of Physical Activity Questionnaire (PPAQ) – initial validation after cultural adaptation.

**Methods** This study applied the ISPOR Principles of Good Practice for Valuation: Drug Diagnostics and other Medical Tests, to perform the translation and adaptation process. The method involved: a) a forward translation, executed by independent linguists, b) a reconciliation by an expert clinical group, c) a review and reconciliation of the back translation executed by the original author, and d) a harmonisation step. Finally, a cognitive debriefing exercise was applied to 20 patients (12 men, mean age of 54.9 ± 17.4 years, suffering from different types of Supra Ventricular Tachycardia).

**Results:** The translation was done systematically and the resulting Polish version of PPAQ was validated using a cognitive debriefing interview. The patients found the PPAQ user-friendly and it seemed to correspond to their experience with seizures. Problems that arose during translation concerned the definition of the particular symptoms (especially the exact wording referring to the sensation of electric current in the muscles), as well as gender difference presented in grammatical structures of the Polish language. These problems were solved after consulting an electrophysi Crime specialist, and due to the suggestions given by the patients during the cognitive debriefing interview.

**Conclusions:** The Polish version of PPAQ proved high initial content validity and was found satisfactory by the patients. It may be recommended for assessment of symptom burden and health related quality of life (HRQoL) in SVT disease in Polish language speaking population. The methods and procedures developed during translation process may be successfully applied to translate the PPAQ into other languages.
